The system employs graph algorithms to map relationships, semantic similarity measure- ments to align enterprise goals with management objectives, and LSTM combined with TF-IDF methods to analyze and recommend specific activities. Experimental results show that this system optimizes IT management practices, enhances operational efficiency, and ensures regulatory compliance. Additionally, a user-friendly graphical representation of internal business pro- cesses using Neo4j enables stakeholders to visualize complex relationships within the COBIT framework, facilitating informed decision-making. This research advances IT governance practices by providing organizations with a valuable tool to automate and improve IT strategies, effectively achieving business objectives. By using Neo4j, organizations can create dynamic and interactive graphical models to better understand and manage the complex relationships between different COBIT 2019 elements . source_paragraph: PREVIOUS WORKS Among the relevant published studies in the log-based anomaly detection space, sequence-based is one of the most commonly used approaches. Therefore, there are limited relevant references as this study employs a point-based approach. Below are the relevant baselines from previous research: 1) Pylogsentiment is the first study which employs sentiment analysis-based anomaly detection on operat- ing system logs using GRU and GloVe embedding. 2) SentiLog , similar to , use a two-layered BiLSTM and GloVe embedding model to perform anomaly detection on parallel file system logs. 3) TransSentLog is a further development of and uses a two-layered transformer encoder which used two attention heads and GloVe embedding along with integrated gradients to add explainability to the trained model.

source_paragraph: I. INTRODUCTION I. INTRODUCTION The use of drones has become increasingly common in various applications, including aerial photography, surveying, and monitoring. However, drones can also be involved in incidents such as accidents, violations, or even crimes, making it necessary to investigate drone logs as part of forensic investigations. In a forensic investigation, drone logs are essential sources of information that can help investigators reconstruct the events leading up to an incident. Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . Important analysis of drone logs involves the identification of the most critical pieces of information in the log data and the analysis of how they relate to the incident

source_paragraph: DATASET The DEAP dataset has 32 participants with a composition of 50% male and 50% female samples . The stimulus used is a video with a duration of 60 seconds. The results of the EEG recording have a duration of 63 seconds, of which 3 seconds are the baseline. One participant has 40 recordings of the EEG trials. The results of the EEG trial recordings have been mea- sured using the Self-Assessment Manikin (SAM). SAM has four emotional dimensions, namely valence, arousal, domi- nance, and liking, with ratings 0-9. For valence and arousal classes, emotion labels are divided into two categories: low and high. In this study, the low class has a rating below 4.5 and the high class is

source_paragraph: Existing anomaly detection modelling techniques can be conducted either in a point, contextual, or collective setting. Point anomaly detection characterises each log entry, whether an event is an abnormality or a normal event. For instance, if a user types the wrong password when trying to gain access to an operating system prompt, the generated log message can be considered as an anomaly , , . As for contextual anomaly, the detection involves analysing some contextually related attributes to determine whether a log entry is an anomaly. If the predicted label is lower than the true label, we define it False Positive (FP). Conversely, if the true label is lower than the predicted label, we define it False Negative (FN). In this context, having less FP is more important than having less FN, as the task objective is anomaly severity detection, where we aim to detect all abnormalities along with the severity level. Therefore, undetected abnormalities, or detecting higher severity as lower ones, indicate that the model fails to meet the task’s objectives. Instead of relying on resource-intensive transfer learning models, our approach optimizes model efficiency, making it more com- putationally friendly or resource-constrained applications. This contribution aims to streamline the implementation of emotion recognition systems without compromising perfor- mance. The proposed research is based on a simplified deep learn- ing architecture and channel selection approach that leads towards closer and resource-constrained real-time implemen- tation. In addition, research for cross-subject validation is more likely to be implemented widely than dependent subject validation. Cross-subject validation has a better generaliza- tion model than dependent-subject validation. CONCLUSION This research demonstrates that the YOLOv8 model, specifically the YOLOv8m variant, provides excellent results in detecting Peritoneal Carcinomatosis (PC) from the used laparoscopic image dataset. By utilizing and fine-tuning the pre-trained YOLOv8 weights, this model shows superior performance with a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 0.871, precision of 0.893, recall of 0.785, and an F1 score of 0.84. The predicted images also show that the performance of YOLOv8 with pre-trained weights is able to predict images well. All YOLOv8 scales are accurate in making predictions, with no errors in image prediction. In contrast, without using pre-trained weights, YOLOv8n and YOLOv8s still make errors in prediction, with some lesions being missed.
